title: invAIriant Audit — orders-api PR #874 (bulk-generated CRUD handlers)
scope: PR #874, a single 1,612-line change adding AI-generated Express/TypeScript route handlers for orders, invoices, and shipments, plus their repos and tests. Audited against docs/security/authz.md and CONTRIBUTING.md. Out of scope: the auth middleware implementation itself (mw/auth.ts), the ORM, and unrelated migrations bundled in the branch.
participants: claude-agent (lens auditor: generated-surface-area, review-bottleneck, parnas, mcconnell)

lens_scores.verdict: near-duplicate handlers generated from one template diverged on the security-relevant line: invoices.ts dropped the requireTenantScope authz middleware present in orders.ts and shipments.ts (GT-001)
1,612-line generated PR approved in a single pass ~9 minutes after push; changed-line test coverage 41% vs the documented 80% floor, and no test exercises the missing-authz case (GT-002)
handler/repo/middleware layering is sound; the defect is a dropped call within an otherwise consistent boundary, not a coupling break (obs O-1)
changes localized and readable, but the near-duplication invites the next generation to add a fourth copy; consolidation not scheduled (obs O-2)

findings.claim: Three route handlers were generated from one template but one diverged on the security-relevant line: the invoices GET handler omits the requireTenantScope authorization middleware that the orders and shipments handlers apply, so any authenticated user can read any tenant's invoice by id.

evidence.description: invoices.get("/:id", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {...}) — the middleware chain has requireAuth but no requireTenantScope("invoices:read"); a code comment even notes the omission
The sibling handler generated from the same template: orders.get("/:id", requireAuth, requireTenantScope("orders:read"), ...) — authz middleware present, establishing the intended pattern
authz.md states 'every tenant-scoped read must pass requireTenantScope before touching a repo'; the invoices handler reaches InvoicesRepo.byId without it

No test in test/routes/invoices.test.ts asserts that a user from tenant A is denied tenant B's invoice; the generated tests assert only 200/404 for the caller's own tenant, so the divergence is invisible to the suite

findings.risk: Cross-tenant data exposure: any authenticated tenant can enumerate and read other tenants' invoices, including totals and billing metadata. The near-duplication hides it — three files look interchangeable, and the one that dropped the check reads identically at a glance.
findings.recommendation: Restore requireTenantScope("invoices:read") on the invoices GET handler. Then remove the class of defect: extract the shared middleware chain into one generated factory so the authz step cannot be dropped per-file, add an arch-conformance test asserting every tenant-scoped route includes requireTenantScope, and add a cross-tenant-denied test for each resource.

verification.method: diffed src/routes/invoices.ts against orders.ts and shipments.ts line by line; confirmed only invoices.ts lacks requireTenantScope; cross-read authz.md:19; grepped test/routes for a cross-tenant assertion (0 hits)
verification.notes: one-line divergence in an otherwise identical template; exactly the near-duplicate-with-small-inconsistency pattern the lens targets

findings.claim: A 1,612-line generated PR was approved in a single pass minutes after push, with changed-line test coverage well below the documented floor and no test covering the authz divergence, so neither human review nor the gates would catch GT-001.

evidence.reference: PR #874 review timeline: pushed 14:02, single approval at 14:11 with no line comments on a 22-file / 1,612-line diff
jest --coverage, CI build 3391: changed-line coverage 41% across src/routes/**; the generated tests mirror the handlers rather than asserting cross-tenant behavior

evidence.description: CONTRIBUTING states 'an 80% changed-line coverage floor applies to all merges'; the CI workflow sets coverage-threshold to 0 for src/routes/**, so the floor is not enforced where the generated code landed
No test exercises the missing-authz path (see GT-001); the deterministic gate that could have caught the divergence — a cross-tenant authz test or an arch-conformance check — does not exist

findings.risk: The highest-volume, least-examined code is also the least-gated: a large generated diff clears review as a formality and clears CI because the coverage floor is disabled on exactly the directory it touched, so a security divergence like GT-001 reaches production unblocked.
findings.recommendation: Restore the 80% changed-line floor for src/routes/** (fix the tests, not the threshold); add an arch-conformance test asserting tenant-scoped routes carry requireTenantScope so a human need not eyeball 22 near-identical files; split future bulk generations into per-resource stacked PRs; record a rubber-stamp metric (approval under N minutes over M changed lines).

verification.method: read the PR #874 timeline and CI build 3391 coverage report; cross-read CONTRIBUTING.md:52 against .github/workflows/ci.yml:39-47 and confirmed the src/routes/** exemption
verification.notes: the gate that would have caught GT-001 is disabled precisely where GT-001 lives

observations.text: Handler/repo/middleware layering is otherwise sound: the fix for GT-001 is a shared middleware factory plus a conformance test, not a re-architecture.
The three resource modules are near-duplicates with no shared abstraction; the next generated resource will likely add a fourth copy. Consolidation is not scheduled.
observations.follow_up: Promote to a finding if a second divergence appears in the duplicated set, or if the copy count grows without a consolidation ticket.

hypotheses.text: The generated repos duplicate SQL and one omits the tenant_id filter, widening the cross-tenant exposure below the handler
hypotheses.rejected_reason: All three repos take tenantId and filter on it: InvoicesRepo.byId(id, tenantId) applies WHERE tenant_id = $2 (src/repos/invoices.ts:22-31), matching orders and shipments. The exposure is solely the missing handler-level scope check (GT-001); the repo layer is consistent. Refuted by reading all three repo files.

hypotheses.text: requireAuth itself is a no-op stub in the generated middleware, so every route is unauthenticated
hypotheses.rejected_reason: mw/auth.ts:14-40 verifies the session token and populates req.tenantId; a passing test test/mw/auth.test.ts:9-27 exercises reject-on-missing-token. requireAuth works; the defect is the missing authorization step, not authentication. Out of audited scope but checked to bound the blast radius.

hypotheses.text: The 1,612-line diff includes speculative dead endpoints shipped 'because they came with the generation'
hypotheses.rejected_reason: Coverage report CI build 3391 shows every generated route has at least one caller in the test suite and the OpenAPI spec references each; no zero-caller export found. Diff mass is high but not dead. Demoted from a candidate dead-surface finding to nothing — no evidence.

summary.executive_summary: PR #874 adds 1,612 lines of near-identical generated CRUD handlers. They read as interchangeable — which is the problem: one of them, invoices.ts, dropped the requireTenantScope authorization middleware that its siblings apply, opening cross-tenant reads of invoice data (GT-001, S1 DIVERGENT_DUPLICATE). It reached main because a 1,612-line generated diff was approved in a single pass minutes after push, and the coverage floor that might have forced a test is disabled on exactly src/routes/** (GT-002, S2 RUBBER_STAMP_MERGE). A generic review of this PR would call it consistent and well-typed; consistency is precisely the camouflage. The layering is otherwise sound (parnas 7), but the S1 blocks the merge.
summary.weakest_reason: near-duplicate handlers with a small, security-relevant inconsistency — the dropped authz check — is the exact defect shape this lens exists to surface (GT-001)

required_actions.action: Restore requireTenantScope("invoices:read") on the invoices GET handler; extract the shared middleware chain into one factory; add an arch-conformance test asserting every tenant-scoped route carries requireTenantScope; add cross-tenant-denied tests per resource
Restore the 80% changed-line coverage floor for src/routes/**; split future bulk generations into stacked per-resource PRs; report a rubber-stamp metric
